ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL JOHN S. LEVITT FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT EARLY SETTLEMENT PROGRAM POLICIES AND PROCEDURES NO. 4. (ADMINISTRATIVE FEE) IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR AND DISSOLVED/FEE PAID. PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED. (610)

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT YMCA OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION OPPOSING THE APPLICATION BY DYNAMO INDUSTRIES, INC. FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT - DYNAMO INDUSTRIES' APPLICATION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS DENIED. DYNAMO INDUSRIES DID NOT SATISFY ITS BURDEN OF PRESENTING COMPETENT NON-CONCLUSORY EVIDENCE OF ALL THE TECH-BILT FACTORS. IN PARTICULAR, APART FROM PAST MEDICAL BILLS, THE ONLY PURPOTED EVIDENCE OF THE AMOUNT OF PLAINTIFF'S DAMAGES AND DYNAMO INDUSTRIES' LIABILITY FOR ANY OR ALL OF THOSE DAMAGES IS THE DECLARATON OF DYNAMO INDUSTRIES' COUNSEL, WHICH IS INSUFFICIENT. (WEIL & BROWN, CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL 12:872-73, CITING, INTER ALIA, GRESHKO V. COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES (1987) 194 CAL. APP 3D 822). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, NOT REPORTED

Off Calendar May-16-2013 Off Calendar

Status Of Filing Of Receipt Of Deposit Of Money Into Blocked Account
Off Calendar Mar-19-2013 Continued To May-21-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Status of filing of Receipt and Acknowledgment of Order for the Deposit of Money into blocked account.
Dept. 514, Judge: John Dearman, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

Nancy M. Conway, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff Elijah Efanov.

Amended Order to Deposit Money into Blocked Account for minor Elija Efanov is signed in open court. Name of the Banking institution isU.S. Bank at 540 Van Ness Ave., San Francisco California.

Status Hearing Scheduled on May 21, 2013 at 9:00 AM, in Department 514 for filing of receipt for the deposit of money into blocked account. No appearance is required if the receipt is filed and courtesy copy lodge with Depart. 514 prior to Hearing.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-26-2013 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (610)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-24-2013 Continued To Feb-26-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Petition To Approve Compromise Of Pending Action Of Minor
Off Calendar Dec-18-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for a Hearing on a Minor's Compromise.
Department 514, Judge: Ellen Chaitin, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.
Nancy M. Conway, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff(s) Petitioner: Helen Mergelian, mother and guardian ad litem for Minor: Elijah Efanov.
Petitioner sworn and examinded.
After testimony and consideration of the evidence presented, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved as follows:
Total Settlement: $25,000.00
Attorney's Fees: $6,250.00 payable to Law Offices of Nancy M. Conway.
Reimbursement for Medical and all other expenses paid by the petitioner or the Petitioner's attorney in the total amount of $512.00 Costs: $91.00
A net balance of $18,147.00 to be deposited into a blocked account at San Francisco Federal Credit Union at Lakeside Village Branch 2645 Ocean Avenue, Suite 1 San Francisco, CA 94132 until Minor reaches the age of 18 years.
Attorney to submit proposed Order approving minor's compromise and Order for Deposit.
Status Hearing Scheduled on March 19, 2013 at 9:00 AM, in Department 514 for filing of receipt for the deposit of money into blocked account. No appearance is required if the receipt is filed and courtesy copy lodge with Depart. 514 prior to Hearing.

MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. CAROL L. HEALY OF THE LAW OFFICES OF BISHOP, BARRY & DRATH, TEL. NO. (510) 596-0888 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT YMCA OF SAN FRANCISCO;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT YMCA OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION OPPOSING THE APPLICATION BY DYNAMO INDUSTRIES, INC. FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT - Dynamo Industries' application for determination of good faith settlement is denied. Dynamo Indusries did not satisfy its burden of presenting competent non-conclusory evidence of all the Tech-Bilt factors. In particular, apart from past medical bills, the only purpoted evidence of the amount of plaintiff's damages and Dynamo Industries' liability for any or all of those damages is the declaraton of Dynamo Industries' counsel, which is insufficient. (Weil & Brown, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial 12:872-73, citing, inter alia, Greshko v. County of Los Angeles (1987) 194 Cal. App 3d 822). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Not reported
